reasoning: I analyzed the FRED time series for Indeed's Software Development job postings. The index has experienced a steady upward recovery over the past 4-5 months, climbing from a localized trough of 61.64 in March 2025 up to 66.26 on August 3, 2025. Given the series is a 7-day trailing average, its short-term momentum (+30 day trend of ~0.068 per day) heavily favors an increase over the initial 7-day forecast horizon (August 10). Because the series is already seasonally adjusted, regular seasonal dips are largely excluded. Over the medium to longer term, uncertainty significantly increases due to conflicting indicators: while the recent 4-month trend is positive, the 12-month trend is negative (with levels having fallen from 69.6 in August 2024). Therefore, my confidence in sustained absolute increases above today's value decays, bringing probabilities for the distant resolution dates (November 2025 and January 2026) gradually closer to 0.5.
